source_title: Introducing FlowSync: Real-Time Collaboration for Remote Engineering Teams

analysis.title: Introducing FlowSync: Real-Time Collaboration for Remote Engineering Teams
analysis.core_message: FlowSync is a new real-time collaboration tool designed specifically for remote engineering teams, combining live code sharing, async video standups, and integrated project tracking.
key_points: Built for engineering teams, not generic collaboration
Live code sharing with syntax highlighting and inline comments
Async video standups — record and watch on your own schedule
Integrated with GitHub, GitLab, Jira, and Linear
End-to-end encrypted — SOC 2 Type II compliant
Free tier for teams up to 5, Pro at $12/user/mo
analysis.target_audience: Engineering managers, remote-first CTOs, developer advocates, tech journalists
analysis.tone: confident, technical but accessible, launch energy
hooks: Slack for chat. Zoom for calls. Nothing built for how engineers actually work.
We built the tool we wished existed for remote engineering.
Real-time code sharing meets async standups.

content.linkedin_post:
We just launched FlowSync. 🚀

After 14 months of building with 500 beta teams, we're live.

FlowSync is real-time collaboration built specifically for remote engineering teams.

What makes it different:

💻 Live code sharing — syntax highlighting, inline comments, pair programming without screen share lag

📹 Async video standups — record your update in 2 minutes, team watches when they're ready. No more scheduling 9 people across 4 time zones.

🔗 Native integrations — GitHub, GitLab, Jira, Linear. Your work context lives where your conversations happen.

🔒 Enterprise ready — end-to-end encrypted, SOC 2 Type II compliant

Pricing:
• Free for teams up to 5
• Pro: $12/user/mo
• Enterprise: custom

Beta results:
→ 4.8/5 satisfaction score
→ 23% reduction in meeting time
→ Teams shipping 15% faster

We built the tool we wished existed.

Try it free → flowsync.dev

#ProductLaunch #RemoteWork #DevTools #Engineering #Startup
twitter_thread:
Introducing FlowSync — real-time collaboration built for how engineers actually work.

We're live today. Here's what we built 🧵
1/ Engineers have Slack, Zoom, Notion, Jira... but nothing designed for engineering workflows. Code sharing still means screen share. Standups still mean meetings.

2/ FlowSync combines:
→ Live code sharing (syntax highlighting, inline comments)
→ Async video standups (record & watch on your schedule)
→ Integration with GitHub, GitLab, Jira, Linear

3/ Built by a remote-first team, for remote-first teams. 500 beta teams tested it for 6 months.

Results:
- 4.8/5 satisfaction
- 23% fewer meetings
- 15% faster shipping

4/ Pricing:
• Free for teams ≤ 5
• Pro: $12/user/mo
• Enterprise: custom

E2E encrypted. SOC 2 Type II.

Try it free → flowsync.dev
content.email_newsletter:
Subject: Introducing FlowSync — built for remote engineering teams

Hey [First Name],

We've been heads-down for 14 months building something we wish existed: a collaboration tool designed specifically for remote engineering teams.

Today we're launching FlowSync.

What it does:
• Live code sharing with syntax highlighting and inline comments — no more laggy screen shares
• Async video standups — record in 2 minutes, team watches on their schedule
• Native integration with GitHub, GitLab, Jira, and Linear

What our 500 beta teams told us:
→ 4.8/5 satisfaction score
→ 23% reduction in meeting time
→ 15% faster feature shipping

Pricing:
• Free for teams up to 5
• Pro: $12/user/month
• Enterprise: custom

Everything is end-to-end encrypted and SOC 2 Type II compliant.

We'd love for you to try it: flowsync.dev

— The FlowSync Team

content.summary_blurb: FlowSync launches as a purpose-built collaboration tool for remote engineering teams, featuring live code sharing, async video standups, and deep integrations with GitHub, GitLab, Jira, and Linear. After 14 months of development with 500 beta teams (4.8/5 satisfaction), it's available free for teams up to 5 and $12/user/month for Pro.
